arguments.task: Research Apify using official first-party pages. Open docs.apify.com and apify.com as needed. Find evidence for: one-line product description; API authentication methods; whether access is self-serve free/trial/paid/gated and why; API surface type (REST/GraphQL/Both/None) and breadth; whether an MCP server/integration exists; buildability for creating an AI-agent toolkit today; rate limit notes; and any notable signals such as changelog, GitHub, or API reference. Prefer official developer docs, API docs, pricing pages, platform docs, GitHub from Apify, and MCP registry pages. Return a concise evidence pack with exact source URLs and quoted facts.

input.thought: Use the browser to inspect official Apify docs and collect evidence for the research schema.

raw_result.description: Platform for ready-to-run scraping and automation tools that AI agents and apps can invoke.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official API docs describe API token auth via Authorization: Bearer <token> or token query parameter. Evidence also indicates 'Agentic Payments' headers (x402 PAYMENT-SIGNATURE / Skyfire skyfire-pay-id) for autonomous paid usage without an Apify account; this is atypical and mapped as other.
raw_result.gating_reason: Self-serve pricing includes a free plan with prepaid usage credits and no credit card requirement; higher limits/features are available on paid tiers but public access is not sales-gated.

api_surface.breadth: Documented HTTP JSON API covering Actors, runs, tasks, storage resources (datasets, key-value stores, request queues), webhooks, schedules, users, and store-related operations.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official MCP integration docs list a hosted server at mcp.apify.com, and Apify maintains an open-source MCP server repository.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official limits docs indicate a global limit of 250,000 requests per minute, a default per-resource limit of 60 requests/second, and higher throughput up to 400 requests/second for some endpoints such as dataset pushes or Actor runs.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official hosted MCP server, open-source apify-mcp-server repo, active GitHub organization, and changelog entries highlighting MCP connectors and agentic payments for AI agents.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Most fields are supported by first-party evidence gathered via browser extraction, but some extracted details rely on summarized browser outputs rather than directly quoted page text in-chat, especially the exact one-line description source wording and the characterization of agentic payments as an auth method.
